name: The Man/Angel
shortDescription: A winged human figure representing intellect, reason, and the element of air, often associated with Saint Matthew in Christian symbolism.
description: In the context of Tarot, the Man/Angel figure, sometimes depicted as Saint Matthew, is one of the four Kerubic creatures (also including the Lion, Bull, and Eagle) that surround the Wheel of Fortune and appear in the World card. This figure is associated with the element of Air, representing intellect, communication, and reason. The appearance is typically of a winged human, often carrying a book or scroll, symbolizing knowledge and divine wisdom. While not possessing specific 'abilities' in the traditional sense, the Angel figure embodies enlightenment, clear thinking, and the power of understanding. The association with Saint Matthew connects it to the Gospel and the written word. Its presence suggests a balanced perspective, the integration of earthly and spiritual realms, and the importance of intellectual pursuits in achieving wholeness. The angel represents the human capacity for rational thought and spiritual insight. The Man/Angel is often seen as a guide, urging individuals to use their intellect and intuition to navigate life's challenges and achieve their full potential. The figure's connection to the Wheel of Fortune underscores the cyclical nature of life and the importance of adapting to change with wisdom and understanding. The association with the World card indicates the completion of a cycle and the attainment of enlightenment through knowledge and experience.
appearance: Winged human figure, often depicted with a book or scroll.

sources.title: The Pictorial Key to the Tarot
sources.author: A.E. Waite

sources.title: Meditations on the Tarot: A Journey into Christian Hermeticism
sources.author: Anonymous

worshipPractices.name: Meditative Gazing
worshipPractices.description: Devotees focus on images of the Man/Angel, particularly within the World or Wheel of Fortune cards. This practice aims to access the entity's associated qualities of intellect, clear thought, and spiritual understanding. It's believed consistent practice can unlock inner wisdom.

worshipPractices.name: Air Element Rituals
worshipPractices.description: Rituals involve invoking the element of Air through controlled breathing, wind chimes, or feather fans. These rituals seek the Man/Angel's blessing for clarity of thought, communication skills, and intellectual pursuits. The rituals are often performed during sunrise or atop high places.

festivals.name: Festival of Ascended Thought
festivals.description: This festival celebrates intellectual and spiritual enlightenment. Scholars and artists gather to share their work, engaging in philosophical debates and creative expressions inspired by the Man/Angel's wisdom. It's a time of reflection and renewed commitment to seeking higher knowledge.

festivals.name: Day of Clear Vision
festivals.description: A day dedicated to seeking guidance and clarity for the coming year. Devotees engage in divination practices, seeking insights into their personal paths and the future, believing the Man/Angel facilitates clear understanding and foresight. Offerings of clear quartz crystals are common.

cultural.modernLegacy: The Man/Angel archetype continues to inspire artists and writers exploring themes of intellect, spiritual awareness, and the pursuit of knowledge. Its imagery frequently appears in modern tarot decks and related artistic interpretations, symbolizing the power of rational thought and higher consciousness. Furthermore, the symbol is a reminder of the importance of balancing intellect with other aspects of life.

cross_cultural_parallels.similarity: Garuda is a bird-like humanoid creature often associated with divine power and knowledge, similar to the angel's higher spiritual connection and association with enlightenment.
The Lamassu, a creature with a human head, bull's body, and eagle's wings, embodies intellect, strength, and vision, mirroring the angel's multifaceted symbolic representation.
Horus, often depicted with a falcon's head, symbolizes protection, kingship, and the power of sight and knowledge, echoing the angel's role as a guardian and a source of wisdom.
Vohu Manah represents Good Thought and divine wisdom. Like the Angel, Vohu Manah embodies a higher spiritual understanding and guides humanity towards righteousness.

associations.name: Eagle
associations.significance: Represents soaring intellect, clear vision, and higher perspective, aligning with the Angel's association with Air and mental clarity.

associations.name: Air
associations.significance: Symbolizes intellect, communication, and the breath of life, mirroring the Angel's role as a messenger and embodiment of mental energy.

associations.name: Yellow
associations.significance: Represents intellect, enlightenment, and mental clarity, reflecting the Angel's connection to knowledge and understanding.
Signifies beginnings, individuality, and focused intent, echoing the Angel's role as a distinct and powerful force.

associations.name: Aquarius
associations.significance: Represents intellect, humanitarianism, and innovation, aligning with the Angel's association with higher knowledge and progress.

associations.name: Enlightenment
associations.significance: Signifies the attainment of knowledge and spiritual understanding, reflecting the Angel's role as a guide towards wisdom.

associations.name: Lavender
associations.significance: Associated with clarity of thought, calmness, and spiritual awareness, mirroring the Angel's presence and influence.

companions.name: The Lion
companions.description: One of the four Kerubic creatures alongside the Man/Angel, the Lion represents the element of Fire and embodies courage and strength. They work together to maintain balance and harmony.

companions.name: The Bull
companions.description: As another Kerubic creature, the Bull symbolizes the element of Earth and signifies stability and groundedness.  The Man/Angel and the Bull represent different facets of worldly experience.

companions.name: The Eagle
companions.description: The Eagle, the final Kerubic creature, embodies the element of Water and signifies intuition and spiritual insight. They complement the Man/Angel's intellect with emotional understanding.

companions.name: Wheel of Fortune
companions.description: A central image in tarot, the Kerubic creatures are often depicted surrounding the Wheel of Fortune, guarding it and representing the cyclical nature of destiny.

companions.name: Saint Matthew
companions.description: The Man/Angel is often associated with Saint Matthew, the apostle and evangelist who is symbolized by a winged man in Christian art. This association links the figure to divine inspiration and the written word.

extendedContent.title: Origin and Nature: The Kerubic Connection
extendedContent.content: The Man/Angel figure in Tarot is deeply rooted in biblical and astrological symbolism, specifically connected to the four Kerubim (cherubim) described in the Book of Ezekiel and the Book of Revelation. These celestial beings, each representing a fixed sign of the zodiac, guard the throne of God. The Man/Angel corresponds to Aquarius, an air sign, embodying intellect, knowledge, and the power of communication. In Ezekiel 1:10, the prophet describes these creatures as having four faces: a man, a lion, an ox, and an eagle. These figures later became associated with the four evangelists in Christian iconography, with the Man often attributed to Saint Matthew, whose gospel emphasizes the humanity of Jesus. The Tarot's incorporation of these Kerubic figures signifies the interplay between divine will and earthly existence, reflecting the cyclical nature of fate and the potential for spiritual enlightenment within worldly experiences. The presence of the Man/Angel suggests a higher level of understanding and awareness, urging individuals to engage their intellect and seek deeper meaning in their journeys. The 'fixed' nature of the zodiacal signs they represent also alludes to stability and the enduring influence of these archetypes.

extendedContent.title: Symbolism and Meaning: Intellect and Divine Communication
extendedContent.content: The primary symbolism of the Man/Angel revolves around intellect, reason, and divine communication. Often depicted with wings, the figure signifies transcendence and the ability to rise above earthly limitations through knowledge and understanding. The book frequently held by the Man/Angel represents wisdom, learning, and the written word as a conduit for divine truth. This symbolizes the importance of seeking knowledge and using one's intellect to decipher the mysteries of the universe. The association with the element of Air further reinforces these themes, highlighting the power of thought, communication, and the interconnectedness of ideas. In the context of the Wheel of Fortune card, the Man/Angel suggests that while fate may play a role, individual intellect and understanding can influence one's response to changing circumstances. On the World card, the presence of the Man/Angel, alongside the other Kerubim, signifies completion and the integration of all aspects of existence – physical, emotional, intellectual, and spiritual – leading to a state of wholeness and enlightenment. This emphasizes the importance of embracing intellectual curiosity and striving for a deeper understanding of oneself and the world.

extendedContent.title: Encounters in Myth: Visions and Revelations
extendedContent.content: While not a central figure in traditional myths in the same way as gods or heroes, the essence of the Man/Angel archetype appears in various stories of divine encounters and revelations. The visions of prophets, such as Ezekiel's description of the Kerubim, can be seen as encounters with this archetype. The angel Gabriel's appearance to Mary in the Christian tradition, announcing the impending birth of Jesus, embodies the element of divine communication associated with the Man/Angel. These encounters often involve the transmission of knowledge or a call to action, highlighting the power of intellect and understanding in shaping one's destiny. In a broader sense, any moment of sudden insight or profound understanding can be interpreted as an encounter with the Man/Angel archetype. These moments are characterized by clarity, a sense of purpose, and the ability to see beyond the immediate circumstances. The Tarot's inclusion of this figure encourages individuals to be open to these moments of revelation and to actively seek knowledge and understanding in their lives, viewing challenges as opportunities for growth and enlightenment.

extendedContent.title: Cultural Significance: Tarot and Beyond
extendedContent.content: The Man/Angel figure holds significant cultural weight, both within the Tarot tradition and in broader artistic and philosophical contexts. Its presence in the Wheel of Fortune and World cards anchors these cards in a rich tradition of symbolism, linking them to biblical, astrological, and philosophical ideas. The figure's association with Saint Matthew also adds a layer of Christian interpretation, connecting it to themes of human compassion and understanding. Beyond Tarot, the archetype of the winged human, representing intellect and spirituality, appears in various forms throughout art and literature. From Renaissance depictions of angels to modern portrayals of enlightened beings, the image resonates with the human desire for transcendence and understanding. The Man/Angel serves as a reminder of the power of intellect, communication, and the pursuit of knowledge in shaping our lives and connecting us to a higher purpose. Its enduring presence in Tarot and other cultural expressions speaks to the timeless appeal of these themes and their relevance to the human experience.

subtitle: Kerubic being representing intellect, communication, and reason.
